emails.body:
NAVY TIMES DAILY HEADLINES
September 30, 2026

TOP STORIES
• BAH rates for 2027 released: average 4.2% increase nationwide, with highest jumps in San Diego, Norfolk, and Hawaii markets
• New PCS order format rolling out FY27: simplified entitlements page, digital weight allowance calculator
• Retention pilot expands: Career Intermission Program now open to O-4 and below with 8+ years service

PERSONNEL & POLICY
• Navy announces FY27 promotion board schedules — O-5 line boards convene January 12
• Updated maternity/convalescent leave policy takes effect Oct 1: 12 weeks standard, up to 14 with CO approval
• Tuition Assistance cap holds at $4,500/year; new STEM degree partnership with Arizona State announced

QUALITY OF LIFE
• Privatized housing: new tenant bill of rights template distributed to all installations
• Commissary surcharge drops to 4.5% starting Oct 1
• MWR launches "Family First Fridays" — free childcare during designated unit events

FLEET & OPERATIONS
• USS Gerald R. Ford completes first full deployment cycle; lessons learned brief to Congress next month
• Unmanned surface vessel squadron establishes at Naval Base Ventura County
• Red Hill defueling: 98% complete, final closure report due December

QUICK LINKS
[View full BAH calculator] [PCS entitlement guide] [Retention program details] [Promotion board precepts]

---

Unsubscribe | Manage preferences | Contact us

NAVY TIMES BENEFITS UPDATE
October 4, 2026

2027 MILITARY PAY TABLES PUBLISHED
The 4.5% across-the-board raise takes effect January 1. Key highlights:
• E-4 over 4: $3,158/month base pay
• O-3 over 6: $7,842/month base pay
• O-5 over 14: $11,093/month base pay
Full tables: dfas.mil/paytables27

FAMILY PROGRAM EXPANSIONS
• Exceptional Family Member Program (EFMP): new respite care voucher — $400/month per enrolled family member, no cost share
• Child Development Center waitlist priority updated: dual-military and single parents now top tier
• Spouse licensing reimbursement increased to $1,000 per move (was $500)

HEALTH & WELLNESS
• TRICARE Select enrollment fee waived for E-1 through E-4 starting Jan 1
• Mental health: embedded providers now at 95% of shore commands; telehealth option permanent
• New postpartum physical training exemption: 12 months post-birth, no profile required

EDUCATION & TRANSITION
• DoD SkillBridge: 200 new industry partners added for FY27
• VA home loan funding fee unchanged; native American direct loan program expanded
• Credentialing Opportunities On-Line (COOL) funding: $4,000 lifetime cap per Sailor

UPCOMING DEADLINES
• Open season for FEDVIP dental/vision: Nov 11 – Dec 9
• Thrift Savings Plan catch-up contributions (age 50+): $7,500 additional for 2027
• Navy College Program application window for spring term: Oct 15 – Nov 15

---
Navy Times Benefits Desk | benefits@navytimes.example
